headline: Malaysian Palm Oil to China Dropped 39% Before Trump Met Xi
context: Malaysia's palm oil exports to China fell 39% in Jan-Oct 2025, six months before Trump met Xi. The plantation minister attributed the drop to price, not geopolitics. Brazil's COFCO signed US$10 billion of competing oilseed agreements in November 2025.

cards.big: Malaysian palm oil to China fell 39% before Trump met Xi, and the cost lands on 210,000 smallholders
cards.sub: Plantation Minister Johari attributed the drop to price (Malaysian CPO at one point USD120 a tonne above soybean oil), not the summit. Mill-gate prices track Bursa CPO directly to smallholder income.

cards.big: Palm oil traded USD120 a tonne above soybean oil at one point in 2025, and Chinese refiners shifted mix
cards.sub: Malaysia's 2024 exports to China were 1.39 million tonnes, down 5.3% on 2023. The Jan-Oct 2025 drop hit 39%. Reuters reported the full-year 2025 China decline at 35.7%.

cards.big: China signed an oilseed deal with Brazil worth more than US$10 billion in November 2025
cards.sub: COFCO inked agreements reported at roughly 20 million tonnes of soybeans, soybean oil and palm oil with ADM, Bunge, Cargill and Louis Dreyfus. Signed contracts, not delivered cargo.

cards.big: Greer announced the deal. China's foreign ministry said working teams were still in consultation
cards.sub: Greer told Bloomberg China would buy 'double-digit billions per year for three years.' Wang Yi said specifics were under consultation. July soybean futures fell 36 cents a bushel that day.

cards.big: When CPO falls, the corporate listcos hedge. Smallholders absorb it
cards.sub: About 210,000 smallholders cultivate ~15% of the planted area. Kenya overtook the EU as #2 buyer in H1 2025. The Oct 2025 US-Malaysia trade deal exempted palm oil. April oleochemicals rose 23.83%.

cards.big: The question is not whether Trump shook palm oil. It is whether Malaysian rerouting outpaces the smallholder cost.
The summit did not cause Malaysia's palm oil problem. Price did, in 2025, before Trump met Xi
cards.sub: Malaysia has been rerouting to Kenya, secured a US tariff exemption, grew oleochemicals 23.83%. The harder story is the gap between trade-policy timetables and smallholder-income timetables.
